role_title: Senior Director, Enterprise Delivery and Growth

location.office_time_required: preference for proximity to Toronto for key cross-functional collaboration

secondary_archetype: Enterprise Customer Success & Delivery Executive (VP/SVP equivalent)

level_signals: Ownership of combined post-sale P&L with $200M+ ARR
Comp range $264K–$334.5K base, consistent with Senior Director at large tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of enterprise software leadership spanning post-sale functions at a B2B SaaS or enterprise technology company.
Direct, accountable leadership of a professional services, implementation, or delivery organization, with demonstrated understanding of org design, quality drivers, and scale failure modes.
Direct, accountable leadership of a customer success or account management organization responsible for post-live growth, NRR, and expansion.
Ownership of a combined post-sale P&L with $200M+ ARR, accountable across the full customer lifecycle (implementation through renewal/expansion).
5+ years managing Director-level leaders across distinct functional disciplines, with a track record of setting standards and holding senior leaders accountable without micromanagement.
Proven success operating in a BD/Sales-led commercial environment and establishing shared norms across BD, Delivery, and CS without imposing rigid process.

Experience leading or directly managing a solutions architecture, technical account management, or professional services engineering function, with an understanding of how technical delivery teams complement commercial account teams on shared enterprise accounts.

Demonstrated ability to integrate technical and commercial organizations under a unified operating model — including solutions architecture, technical account management, and engineering functions alongside commercial success teams — while preserving functional depth and career paths within each discipline.
Deep familiarity with enterprise retail, grocery, or adjacent complex B2B domains, including how large retailers evaluate, implement, and scale technology solutions.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Track record of improving time-to-live and implementation quality at scale, with measurable gains in launch speed, activation rates, or delivery consistency.
Experience transforming CS from reactive enablement to proactive business advisory, including talent strategy, operating model, and tooling changes.
Hands-on design of implementation-to-success motions where delivery and success run in parallel on the same accounts.

Experience running commercial success and technical account management as distinct disciplines within the same organization, with clear role boundaries, shared account ownership models, and escalation protocols.

Track record of building or scaling a TAM or technical success function — including defining the role boundary between TAM, solutions architecture, and support — with clear career ladders that attract and retain technical talent.
Background operating across multiple software business lines with distinct GTM and delivery motions and establishing cross-portfolio coordination standards.
Familiarity with tiered CS models (high-touch for strategic accounts and scaled/digital for the long tail) and when to apply each.
Experience applying AI to augment CS, TAM, and delivery workflows to scale coverage and quality without linear headcount growth.
Background in or strong exposure to grocery, retail technology, ecommerce platforms, or supply chain software.

scope.team_size_estimate: Three Director-led organizations (Retailer Delivery, Enterprise Architecture & Engineering, Retailer Success)
scope.surface: StorefrontPro, Enterprise API (Fulfillment and Ads), FoodStorm — full enterprise software portfolio post-sale lifecycle
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / enterprise software, retailer-facing digital commerce platform
core_responsibilities: Own business outcomes end to end for StorefrontPro, Enterprise API, and FoodStorm including NRR, expansion GTV, gross revenue retention, time-to-live, and post-live adoption velocity
Lead and set strategic agenda for Retailer Delivery, Enterprise Architecture & Engineering, and Retailer Success Director-led teams
Build senior consultative CS capability by hiring and sponsoring Principal Growth Strategists
Define and operationalize joint BD-CS operating model for enterprise accounts
Set strategic standard for enterprise implementations and champion tooling, automation, and platform investments
Design implementation-to-success motion as an intentionally managed lifecycle
Lead Enterprise Architecture & Engineering organization to drive technical delivery excellence and API adoption
Represent portfolio narrative to executive leadership and maintain executive presence with retailer counterparts

compensation.equity_form: New hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across all US geographies
Equity (new hire + annual refresh grants) explicitly mentioned
Fully remote role across US and Canada
Clear ownership of three Director-led organizations with well-defined scope
Specific metrics named (NRR, GTV, time-to-live) indicating analytical rigor in the organization
Flex First remote work policy in place
red_flags: Preference for proximity to Toronto creates soft geographic constraint despite remote designation
Very high ARR threshold ($200M+) for prior P&L ownership may screen out many otherwise qualified candidates
Role spans implementation, CS, and technical engineering — extremely broad scope that may create execution risk

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, org scope, and 12+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and retailer-facing context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not enumerated as a discrete list in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd lower/upper (stated as ranges by geography; $264K–$334.5K represents the full band across all US geographies)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from #LI-Remote tag and 'remote across the United States and Canada' statement)
ambiguities: Role is described as 'remote across the United States and Canada' but also states 'preference for proximity to Toronto' — unclear whether this is a soft preference or a meaningful constraint; treated as soft preference here.
Compensation ranges are provided only for US-based candidates; Canada compensation not stated despite role being open to Canadian candidates.
Visa sponsorship eligibility not addressed anywhere in the JD.
